prompt: You are reviewing the R package in the current working directory for an rOpenSci software-review issue. Analyse its code complexity and produce a report I can attach to the review issue. I want to trust the report, so measure rather than eyeball, and back every finding with evidence.
assertions: Reports measured cyclomatic complexity per function
Measures more than one metric (LOC, nesting, or if/for counts)
Builds or uses a static call-graph / duplication analysis
Cites concrete file/line evidence for hotspots
Reproduces the triggering prompt in the report
Names the skill/method that produced the report
States no package files were modified (read-only)
Recommends simplifications that keep the user-facing surface unchanged
Confirms a behavioural finding with a runtime probe
